name: Gemfury
license: Proprietary, closed-source SaaS. The legacy Ruby client (github.com/gemfury/gemfury) is MIT-licensed; the current Go CLI (github.com/gemfury/cli) source is public on GitHub but ships no license file.
cost: Free 'Public' plan ($0/mo, unlimited public packages, no private packages). Personal plans scale by collaborator count with unlimited private packages: $9/mo (1 collaborator), $14/mo (5), $23/mo (10), $50/mo (25), $100/mo (50). Team plans scale by private-package count with unlimited collaborators: $25/mo (10 private) up to $650/mo (450 private). 14-day free trial, no credit card. Enterprise/custom pricing via hello@gemfury.com.
language: Proprietary/undisclosed service backend (Ruby/Rails heritage); native CLI in Go; legacy API client in Ruby
last_updated: 2024-07-20 (native Go CLI v0.23.0, latest GitHub release); SaaS actively maintained as of 2026 (uv install guide, passkey auth)
perf_notes: No published throughput/latency/memory benchmarks. Managed multi-tenant cloud service; all delivery over SSL/HTTPS. Performance is vendor-operated and not customer-tunable.
scale_notes: SaaS-only; vendor runs the HA/scaled infrastructure, so there is no customer-side clustering, replication, or node sizing. Not self-hostable. Pricing scales by collaborators (Personal) or private-package count (Team), not by storage/bandwidth. No published per-account scale limits.
summary: A hosted, multi-ecosystem private package registry (PyPI/RubyGems/npm/NuGet/Composer/Go/Maven/DEB/RPM/Bower) that lets teams publish and install private and public packages without running their own server.
extras: Multi-ecosystem registry in one account: RubyGems, PyPI, npm/Yarn, Composer (PHP), NuGet (.NET), Go modules, Maven (Java), Debian/apt (DEB), RPM/yum, Bower
Hosts public packages for free (acts as a public distribution index, not just private)
GPG-signed apt (DEB) and yum (RPM) Linux repositories
Authentication options: API/deploy (read) tokens, push (write) tokens, collaborator tokens with custom permissions and expiry, SAML SSO, Sign in with Google, passkeys, member 2FA visibility
Organization accounts plus an impersonation/collaborator model for team access (--as flag, account switcher)
Native Go CLI (`fury`: push, list, versions, yank, migrate) plus legacy Ruby gem client
Invite system with role assignment and expiry
uv support: configure pypi.fury.io/ACCOUNT/ as an index in pyproject.toml; uv resolves Gemfury first and falls back to public PyPI
`fury yank` permanently removes a package version (a hard delete, not a PEP 592 soft-yank)
